generated_note: Written by Claude Code during a review session, not auto-generated on a timer — ask Claude to refresh this after logging a batch of new flights or if a minimum crosses a milestone.

verify_first.label: Resolve this before anything else
verify_first.body: Whether Endeavor's SIC-combination rule (the asterisk on PIC / PIC Cross Country / PIC Night) covers your military SIC time. If it does: PIC (318.5/250), PIC Night (40.2/25), and PIC Cross Country (297.6/100) are all already met. If it doesn't: PIC sits at 17.2/250 (7%) — by far the hardest number on this page to move through personal flying alone. This one answer determines whether your three biggest categories are done or are your entire remaining bottleneck.

stalled.label: Stalled — needs a deliberate decision
stalled.body: Multi-Engine Land: 13.8/25 (55%), unchanged since your last DA-42 session in June 2025. Nothing in your current single-engine training path touches this — it will not self-resolve. Needs booking more multi-engine time specifically.

resolving_naturally.label: Resolving on its own — don't force it
resolving_naturally.body: Instrument (43.2/75) and the rest of Cross Country (already past the 200-hr Restricted-ATP floor at 306.6/500) will move through the normal Instrument rating syllabus. No need to chase these separately.

highest_leverage.label: Highest-leverage move for total hours
highest_leverage.body: Commercial → CFI. Current pace is roughly 83 hrs in 4 months (~20 hrs/month); at that rate 750 total time is over a year out. Instructing accumulates PIC and total hours several times faster than personal rental flying — and pays you instead of costing you. If speed to Endeavor matters, this is the path, not more solo XC in a rented 172.

lagging.label: Lagging without a natural fix
lagging.body: Night: 44.6/100 (45%). Unlike Instrument, no certificate specifically forces night cross-country time — it only accumulates incidentally. Worth scheduling a couple of dedicated night XC legs rather than waiting for it.
